results.possibleIdentity: Arthur Burg was an electrical engineer who was active during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was employed by the Bendix Corporation and based in New York, NY. Burg was a member of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization established in 1966 to foster collaborations between the scientific/engineering communities and artists. His professional details, including his association with Bendix and his expertise in electrical engineering, were recorded in the technical membership directories of E.A.T., which maintained a registry of engineers available to consult with artists on various technical projects.
